title: cornus canadensis f. purpurascens
intro: Cornus canadensis f. purpurascens, also known as creeping dogwood, is a flowering plant in the cornus genus. It is a type of shrub with red stems and white, greenish, or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. It is used in herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing stress and improving immune function.
